name: transfer
description: Enterprise-grade unified import/export engine. Thin wrapper around `maatwebsite/excel` with our conventions: attribute-driven registration (#[Importable] / #[Exportable] / #[SampleData] / #[ImportField] / #[ExportField] / #[TransferField] / #[ImportableWorkbook] / #[ExportableWorkbook]), queue-by-default execution riding Laravel Excel's chain + WithChunkReading, an `xfer_jobs` domain record persisted independently of the queue lifetime (the operation, not the queue job), four import modes (append / upsert / replace / delete), sharded imports for very large files, downloadable errors artifact for partial success, signed download URLs, workspace + branch scoping via BelongsToWorkspace, per-user notifications on finish (mail / broadcast / database / push) resolved at dispatch, mapping profiles for header remap at upload time, and a preview + dry-run pipeline. Distinct from `activity` (product feed) and `audit` (compliance evidence) — transfer runs the data-movement operations both of those modules observe. Foundation's earlier `Importable` / `Exportable` traits + `ImportContractRegistry` / `ExportContractRegistry` bindings + `ProcessImportBatchJob` / `GenerateExportJob` moved here as of this module's inception; foundation retains no import/export mentions.

wraps.rationale: Battle-tested Excel + CSV + PDF reader / writer with queue chaining, chunk reading, batch inserts, custom CSV settings, and event hooks. We stand on top: `AbstractDynamicImport` / `AbstractDynamicExport` extend their base concerns and layer attribute-driven registration, xfer_jobs domain record, error artifact writer, mode-based imports (append / upsert / replace / delete), and per-user notifications on completion. Their `->queue()` chain, `WithChunkReading`, `WithBatchInserts`, `WithCustomChunkSize`, `WithCustomCsvSettings`, `WithHeadings`, `WithMapping`, `WithValidation`, `WithUpserts`, `SkipsFailures`, `FromQuery`, `FromView`, `FromGenerator`, `FromCollection`, `WithMultipleSheets`, `WithMappedCells`, `WithDrawings`, `WithColumnWidths`, `WithColumnFormatting`, `WithStartRow`, `WithCustomQuerySize`, `HasLocalePreference`, and job `middleware()` mechanisms are used verbatim — we never re-implement queueing or chunking.

foundation.reason: Foundation is pure infrastructure with zero domain entities. Import / export is a data-movement domain in its own right — it owns entities (xfer_jobs, xfer_shards, xfer_artifacts, xfer_mapping_profiles), HTTP surfaces, notifications, retention policies, and third-party wrap semantics. Same rationale as splitting `audit` and `activity` off from foundation instead of embedding them there.

workspace.description: Workspace surface: initiate imports / exports / sample generation, list + cancel + inspect one's own jobs, download completed artifacts (signed URLs), manage saved mapping profiles.

central_admin.description: Platform-admin cross-workspace view of transfer jobs for support triage + capacity planning. No mutation — admins observe, they don't run workspace imports.

reuses.notifications: Every job completion / partial success / failure produces a Laravel Notification via the notifications module. Channels (mail / broadcast / database / push) resolved once at dispatch from xfer_job.notify_channels — audit-clean.
reuses.activity: Every xfer_job status transition writes an activity row via HasActivityLog on the XferJob model. Workspace admins see 'Alice imported 1,245 athletes' in the feed.
reuses.audit: Every xfer_job status transition also writes an audit row via HasAudit. Compliance surface distinct from the product feed.
